heading: 10.0.1 Stoke’s Theorem
text: Let \(S\) piecewise smooth oriented surface space, let boundary \(S\) piecewise smooth simple closed curve \(C\). \(\mathbf{F}(x, y, z)\) continuous vector function continuous first partial derivatives domain containing \(S\), :\[\iint_S (\text{curl } \mathbf{F}) \cdot \mathbf{n} \, dA = \oint_C \mathbf{F} \cdot \mathbf{r}'(s) \, ds\]formula:\(\mathbf{n}\) unit normal vector \(S\).\(\mathbf{r}'(s)\) unit tangent vector \(C\).\(s\) arc length \(C\).integration around \(C\) performed sense consistent orientation normal vector \(\mathbf{n}\), following right-hand rule.

heading: 10.0.3 Double Integral
text:
double integral defined following identity:
\[\iint_R f(x, y) \, dA = \lim_{m, n \\infty} \sum_{=1}^m \sum_{j=1}^n f(x_{ij}^*, y_{ij}^*) \Delta \]\(R\) closed bounded region \(xy\)-plane function integrated [1, 2].\(m\) \(n\) number subrectangles region \(R\) partitioned [3, 4].\(R_{ij}\) smaller subrectangles resulting dividing \(R\) using grid lines parallel axes [4, 5].\(\Delta \) represents area subrectangle \(R_{ij}\) [4, 6].\((x_{ij}^*, y_{ij}^*)\) arbitrary sample point chosen within subrectangle \(R_{ij}\) [4, 6].limit taken \(m\) \(n\) approach infinity maximum diameter (greatest distance two points within subregion) approaches zero [3, 7].function \(f(x, y)\), integrand, typically assumed continuous \(R\) ensure limit exists independent choice sample points [7, 8].

heading: 10.0.4 Double Integral Notational Variations
text:
exist double integral two equivalent notations:
* Differential Area Form: \(\displaystyle \iint_R f(x, y) \, dA\).
* Coordinate Form: \(\displaystyle \iint_R f(x, y) \, dx \, dy\).

heading: 13 Symmetry
text:
Symmetry invariance coordinate systems.definition (Symmetry) property tensor matrix components remain unchanged indices interchanged object transposed, satisfying following conditions:(Tensor Component Form) second-rank tensor \(\), symmetric \(A_{mn} = A_{nm}\) \(m\) \(n\).(Matrix Transposition) matrix symmetric unchanged transposition, \(\tilde{} = \).(Resolution Property) Every second-rank tensor can resolved symmetric part, given \(\frac{1}{2}(A_{mn} + A_{nm})\), antisymmetric part.
- \(\) tensor matrix.
- \(m, n\) indices identifying components.
- \(\tilde{}\) (\(^T\)) transpose matrix.definition (Antisymmetry) property tensor matrix components change sign indices interchanged, satisfying following conditions:(Tensor Component Form) second-rank tensor \(\), antisymmetric \(A_{mn} = -A_{nm}\) \(m\) \(n\).(Diagonal Property) antisymmetric matrix, diagonal elements (\(m=n\)) must zero.(Dual Association) 3-D space, every antisymmetric second-rank tensor \(C\) can associated dual pseudovector \(\mathbf{C}\).
- \(\) tensor matrix.
- \(m, n\) indices identifying components.
- \(C\) antisymmetric tensor.definition (Permutational Antisymmetry = Fermion Antisymmetry) property identical-particle systems, specifically fermions, whose wave functions must change sign pairwise particle interchanges, satisfying following conditions:(Permutation Condition) many-fermion wave function \(\Psi_F(1, \dots, n)\) must satisfy \(P\Psi_F(1, \dots, n) = \epsilon_P\Psi_F(1, \dots, n)\) permutation \(P\).(Representation Role) \(\Psi_F\) serves sole basis function totally antisymmetric representation symmetric group \(S_n\).(Levi-Civita Relation) sign transformation determined Levi-Civita symbol \(\epsilon_P\), \(\epsilon_P = 1\) even permutations \(\epsilon_P = -1\) odd permutations.
- \(\Psi_F\) fermion wave function.
- \(P\) permutation operator acting particle numbers.
- \(\epsilon_P\) parity permutation.
- \(S_n\) symmetric group order \(n!\).

text: Forms functions take vectors input return scalars. 1-form takes single vector input, 2-form takes two vectors input, .definition (1-form = linear functional = covector = covariant vector) linear, real-valued function single vector input, \(\boldsymbol{\omega}: \mathbf{V} \rightarrow \mathbb{R}\), satisfies following conditions vectors scalars:(Additivity) \(\boldsymbol{\omega}(\mathbf{u} + \mathbf{v}) = \boldsymbol{\omega}(\mathbf{u}) + \boldsymbol{\omega}(\mathbf{v})\).(Homogeneity) \(\boldsymbol{\omega}(k\mathbf{v}) = k \cdot \boldsymbol{\omega}(\mathbf{v})\).\(\mathbf{V}\) real vector space.\(\mathbb{R}\) set real numbers.\(\boldsymbol{\omega}\) 1-form.\(\mathbf{u}, \mathbf{v}\) vectors \(\mathbf{V}\).\(k\) scalar.definition (2-form) bilinear, antisymmetric, real-valued function two vector inputs, \(\boldsymbol{\omega}: \mathbf{V} \times \mathbf{V} \rightarrow \mathbb{R}\), satisfies following conditions vectors scalars:(Bilinearity) \(\boldsymbol{\omega}(\mathbf{u} + b\mathbf{u}', \mathbf{v}) = \cdot \boldsymbol{\omega}(\mathbf{u}, \mathbf{v}) + b \cdot \boldsymbol{\omega}(\mathbf{u}', \mathbf{v})\) \(\boldsymbol{\omega}(\mathbf{u}, \mathbf{v} + b\mathbf{v}') = \cdot \boldsymbol{\omega}(\mathbf{u}, \mathbf{v}) + b \cdot \boldsymbol{\omega}(\mathbf{u}, \mathbf{v}')\).(Antisymmetry) \(\boldsymbol{\omega}(\mathbf{u}, \mathbf{v}) = -\boldsymbol{\omega}(\mathbf{v}, \mathbf{u})\).\(\mathbf{V}\) real vector space.\(\mathbb{R}\) set real numbers.\(\boldsymbol{\omega}\) 2-form.\(\mathbf{u}, \mathbf{u}', \mathbf{v}, \mathbf{v}'\) vectors \(\mathbf{V}\).\(, b\) scalars.definition (k-form) multilinear, completely antisymmetric, real-valued function \(k\) vector inputs, \(\boldsymbol{\omega}: \mathbf{V}^k \rightarrow \mathbb{R}\), satisfies following conditions vectors scalars:(k-Linearity) \(\boldsymbol{\omega}(\mathbf{v}_1, \dots, \mathbf{v}_i + b\mathbf{v}_i', \dots, \mathbf{v}_k) = \cdot \boldsymbol{\omega}(\mathbf{v}_1, \dots, \mathbf{v}_i, \dots, \mathbf{v}_k) + b \cdot \boldsymbol{\omega}(\mathbf{v}_1, \dots, \mathbf{v}_i', \dots, \mathbf{v}_k)\) argument \(\) (k-Linearity) \(\boldsymbol{\omega}(\mathbf{v}_1, \dots, \mathbf{v}_i + b\mathbf{v}_i', \dots, \mathbf{v}_k) = \cdot \boldsymbol{\omega}(\mathbf{v}_1, \dots, \mathbf{v}_i, \dots, \mathbf{v}_k) + b \cdot \boldsymbol{\omega}(\mathbf{v}_1, \dots, \mathbf{v}_i', \dots, \mathbf{v}_k)\) argument \(\) \(1 \leq \leq k\).\(1 \leq \leq k\).(Complete Antisymmetry) Swapping two vector inputs reverses sign output: \(\boldsymbol{\omega}(\dots, \mathbf{u}, \dots, \mathbf{v}, \dots) = -\boldsymbol{\omega}(\dots, \mathbf{v}, \dots, \mathbf{u}, \dots)\).(Complete Antisymmetry) Swapping two vector inputs reverses sign output: \(\boldsymbol{\omega}(\dots, \mathbf{u}, \dots, \mathbf{v}, \dots) = -\boldsymbol{\omega}(\dots, \mathbf{v}, \dots, \mathbf{u}, \dots)\).\(\mathbf{V}\) real vector space.\(\mathbf{V}^k\) \(k\)-fold Cartesian product \(\mathbf{V}\).\(\mathbb{R}\) set real numbers.\(\boldsymbol{\omega}\) \(k\)-form.\(\mathbf{v}_1, \dots, \mathbf{v}_k, \mathbf{v}_i',
